message:
fix(engine): harden CLI commands with strict input validation and fail-closed test coverage

Tighten input validation on two CLI entry points so they reject invalid
or missing arguments instead of silently proceeding with empty defaults:

runtime_diagnostics ga-evidence-package:
- Require --release-candidate <id> to be present and non-empty
  (previously accepted an empty string via unwrap_or_default)
- Filter out whitespace-only values with .filter(|v| !v.trim().is_empty())
- Add ga_evidence_package_command_requires_release_candidate integration
  test asserting the command fails with a clear error message when the
  flag is omitted

frankenctl replay:
- Add frankenctl_replay_unfinished_trace_fails_closed integration test
  verifying that replaying a trace that was never finalised produces a
  non-zero exit code and a "trace is not finalised" error message
  (companion test for the trace finalization enforcement in
  deterministic_replay.rs committed earlier)

Both changes follow the fail-closed principle: invalid or incomplete
inputs produce clear errors rather than proceeding with defaults that
could mask problems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
